mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic slowdown, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or t-statistic for return difference is > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05 across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during recovery phases, with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM consistently precede positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms macroeconomic resilience, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP prints above 2.5% are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases during late-cycle phases, with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment indicate synchronized supply and labor market strength, creating a positive feedback loop that pushes S&P 500 returns higher over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals from INDPRO and PAYEMS yield negative 5-day forward returns with a t-statistic less than -1.5.
